name: Gangwon (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: purity and the bright future of the province
the East Sea, which forms Gangwon's eastern coast

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem combining a blue mountain or wave form, a green leaf-like form and a red curve, with the wordmark 'GANGWON' below.
history: Gangwon Province (Gangwon-do), spanning Korea's east coast from the Demilitarized Zone south to the central Taebaek Mountains, has used several official symbols. Following its reorganisation as the Gangwon Special Self-Governing Province on 11 June 2023, an updated provincial flag and emblem were introduced. The motif represents the Taebaek Mountains, the East Sea, and the province's vitality.

features.symbolism: blue mountain-and-wave form (Taebaek Mountains and East Sea) combined with a green leaf and red curve, representing nature, vitality and the dynamic future of Gangwon
